model.dbt_pipeline.agg_hours_per_project.name: agg_hours_per_project
model.dbt_pipeline.agg_hours_per_project.description: Per-project rollup of logged hours from the clean fact table. project_id is a database-enforced primary key (the aggregation grain).
model.dbt_pipeline.agg_hours_per_project.raw_sql:
-- agg_hours_per_project
-- Total hours per project from the clean fact mart. Reads ONLY from
-- fct_timesheets (single source of accepted, enriched facts); no re-joins.
-- project_id uniqueness (the aggregation grain) is asserted here.

select
    project_id,
    project_name,
    count(*)::integer                     as timesheet_count,
    sum(hours)::decimal(12, 2)            as total_hours,
    count(distinct employee_id)::integer  as distinct_employees
from {{ ref('fct_timesheets') }}
group by project_id, project_name
order by total_hours desc
model.dbt_pipeline.agg_hours_per_project.compiled_sql:
-- agg_hours_per_project
-- Total hours per project from the clean fact mart. Reads ONLY from
-- fct_timesheets (single source of accepted, enriched facts); no re-joins.
-- project_id uniqueness (the aggregation grain) is asserted here.

select
    project_id,
    project_name,
    count(*)::integer                     as timesheet_count,
    sum(hours)::decimal(12, 2)            as total_hours,
    count(distinct employee_id)::integer  as distinct_employees
from "dbt_pipeline"."main_marts"."fct_timesheets"
group by project_id, project_name
order by total_hours desc

columns.name: timesheet_count
columns.description: Number of timesheet entries for this project.

columns.name: total_hours
columns.description: Total logged hours for this project (sum of fct_timesheets.hours).

columns.name: distinct_employees
columns.description: Count of distinct employees who logged hours on this project.

model.dbt_pipeline.agg_hours_per_project.name: agg_hours_per_project_monthly
model.dbt_pipeline.agg_hours_per_project.description: Monthly per-project rollup of logged hours. Adds a time dimension to the per-project aggregation, exposing each project's burn-down over time. One row per (project_id, month). Reads fct_timesheets.
model.dbt_pipeline.agg_hours_per_project.raw_sql:
-- agg_hours_per_project_monthly
-- Monthly hours per project (project x month). Adds the TIME dimension to the
-- per-project rollup, revealing each project's burn-down / tempo over time --
-- a flat annual total hides this. Derives the month from the fact date directly
-- (no dim_date needed). One row per (project_id, month). Reads only fct_timesheets.

select
    project_id,
    project_name,
    cast(date_trunc('month', date) as date) as month,
    count(*)::integer                    as timesheet_count,
    sum(hours)::decimal(12, 2)           as total_hours,
    count(distinct employee_id)::integer as distinct_employees
from {{ ref('fct_timesheets') }}
group by project_id, project_name, cast(date_trunc('month', date) as date)
order by project_id, month
model.dbt_pipeline.agg_hours_per_project.compiled_sql:
-- agg_hours_per_project_monthly
-- Monthly hours per project (project x month). Adds the TIME dimension to the
-- per-project rollup, revealing each project's burn-down / tempo over time --
-- a flat annual total hides this. Derives the month from the fact date directly
-- (no dim_date needed). One row per (project_id, month). Reads only fct_timesheets.

select
    project_id,
    project_name,
    cast(date_trunc('month', date) as date) as month,
    count(*)::integer                    as timesheet_count,
    sum(hours)::decimal(12, 2)           as total_hours,
    count(distinct employee_id)::integer as distinct_employees
from "dbt_pipeline"."main_marts"."fct_timesheets"
group by project_id, project_name, cast(date_trunc('month', date) as date)
order by project_id, month

columns.description: Project identifier; part of the composite primary key (project_id, month).

columns.name: month
columns.description: First day of the month (date_trunc('month', date)); part of the composite primary key.

insights.generated_description: Timestamp recording when the month occurred.

columns.name: timesheet_count
columns.description: Number of timesheet entries for this project in this month.

columns.name: total_hours
columns.description: Total logged hours for this project in this month.

columns.name: distinct_employees
columns.description: Count of distinct employees who logged hours on this project in this month.

model.dbt_pipeline.dim_employees.name: dim_employees
model.dbt_pipeline.dim_employees.description: Employee dimension. One row per employee_id, drawn from the deduplicated staging survivors. Contracted; employee_id is a database-enforced primary key and the foreign-key target for fct_timesheets.
model.dbt_pipeline.dim_employees.raw_sql:
-- dim_employees
-- Clean employee dimension: one row per employee_id from stg_employees (the
-- dedup survivors, non-null id). Soft-issue rows (null name/role) are retained
-- since the id is valid. Contracted FK target with the PK test. Reads only
-- stg_employees (single atomic source).

select
    employee_id,
    name,
    role
from {{ ref('stg_employees') }}
model.dbt_pipeline.dim_employees.compiled_sql:
-- dim_employees
-- Clean employee dimension: one row per employee_id from stg_employees (the
-- dedup survivors, non-null id). Soft-issue rows (null name/role) are retained
-- since the id is valid. Contracted FK target with the PK test. Reads only
-- stg_employees (single atomic source).

select
    employee_id,
    name,
    role
from "dbt_pipeline"."main_staging"."stg_employees"

columns.name: role
columns.description: Employee role; nullable. One of a fixed set of valid roles.

model.dbt_pipeline.dim_projects.name: dim_projects
model.dbt_pipeline.dim_projects.description: Project dimension. One row per project_id, drawn from the deduplicated staging survivors. Contracted; project_id is a database-enforced primary key and the foreign-key target for fct_timesheets.
model.dbt_pipeline.dim_projects.raw_sql:
-- dim_projects
-- Clean project dimension: one row per project_id from stg_projects (the
-- dedup survivors, non-null id). Negative budget is nullified here (the
-- consumer-facing dim). Soft-issue rows (null project_name, null/invalid
-- budget) are retained since the id is valid. Contracted FK target with the
-- PK test. Reads only stg_projects (single atomic source).

select
    project_id,
    project_name,
    case when budget < 0 then null else budget end as budget
from {{ ref('stg_projects') }}
model.dbt_pipeline.dim_projects.compiled_sql:
-- dim_projects
-- Clean project dimension: one row per project_id from stg_projects (the
-- dedup survivors, non-null id). Negative budget is nullified here (the
-- consumer-facing dim). Soft-issue rows (null project_name, null/invalid
-- budget) are retained since the id is valid. Contracted FK target with the
-- PK test. Reads only stg_projects (single atomic source).

select
    project_id,
    project_name,
    case when budget < 0 then null else budget end as budget
from "dbt_pipeline"."main_staging"."stg_projects"

model.dbt_pipeline.fct_timesheets.name: fct_timesheets
model.dbt_pipeline.fct_timesheets.description: Timesheets fact table: the accepted fact set joined to dim_employees and dim_projects with dimension attributes denormalized for query convenience. One row per timesheet_id. Primary key enforced by the database; foreign-key integrity is enforced upstream by int_timesheets_validated and asserted here by relationships tests.
model.dbt_pipeline.fct_timesheets.raw_sql:
-- fct_timesheets
-- The clean timesheets fact mart: the accepted fact set from
-- int_timesheets_validated joined to dim_employees / dim_projects. Pure join,
-- no filters -- int already removed dangling FKs and single-source rejects, so
-- the grain is clean (one row per timesheet_id). PK (timesheet_id) and FK
-- (employee_id -> dim_employees, project_id -> dim_projects) tests run directly
-- on the columns here with no `where` filtering.

select
    ts.timesheet_id,
    ts.employee_id,
    emp.name as employee_name,
    emp.role as employee_role,
    ts.project_id,
    prj.project_name,
    prj.budget as project_budget,
    ts.date,
    ts.hours
from {{ ref('int_timesheets_validated') }} ts
left join {{ ref('dim_employees') }} emp on emp.employee_id = ts.employee_id
left join {{ ref('dim_projects') }}  prj on prj.project_id  = ts.project_id
model.dbt_pipeline.fct_timesheets.compiled_sql:
-- fct_timesheets
-- The clean timesheets fact mart: the accepted fact set from
-- int_timesheets_validated joined to dim_employees / dim_projects. Pure join,
-- no filters -- int already removed dangling FKs and single-source rejects, so
-- the grain is clean (one row per timesheet_id). PK (timesheet_id) and FK
-- (employee_id -> dim_employees, project_id -> dim_projects) tests run directly
-- on the columns here with no `where` filtering.

select
    ts.timesheet_id,
    ts.employee_id,
    emp.name as employee_name,
    emp.role as employee_role,
    ts.project_id,
    prj.project_name,
    prj.budget as project_budget,
    ts.date,
    ts.hours
from "dbt_pipeline"."main_intermediate"."int_timesheets_validated" ts
left join "dbt_pipeline"."main_marts"."dim_employees" emp on emp.employee_id = ts.employee_id
left join "dbt_pipeline"."main_marts"."dim_projects"  prj on prj.project_id  = ts.project_id

columns.name: timesheet_id
columns.description: Fact primary key (DB-enforced). Deterministic md5 surrogate of the natural key (employee_id, project_id, date, hours).

columns.name: date
columns.description: Timesheet date; non-null. Parsed from multiple source formats.

insights.generated_description: Timestamp recording when the date occurred.

model.dbt_pipeline.int_timesheets_valida.name: int_timesheets_validated
model.dbt_pipeline.int_timesheets_valida.description: The accepted fact set: stg_timesheets semi-joined to stg_employees and stg_projects to retain only rows whose foreign keys resolve to a real dimension row. The only cross-source model in the pipeline; feeds fct_timesheets.
model.dbt_pipeline.int_timesheets_valida.raw_sql:
-- int_timesheets_validated
-- The cross-source fact-cleaning intermediate: connects stg_timesheets to the
-- clean stg_employees / stg_projects and filters dangling FKs -> the accepted
-- fact set. This is the legitimate intermediate role (joins/references multiple
-- atomic staging models). Uses semi-joins (IN) against the clean dim-source
-- keys to avoid fan-out. No flags, no status column -- cleaning is a where
-- filter, not a flag.

select
    ts.timesheet_id,
    ts.employee_id,
    ts.project_id,
    ts.date,
    ts.hours
from {{ ref('stg_timesheets') }} ts
where ts.employee_id in (select employee_id from {{ ref('stg_employees') }} where employee_id is not null)
  and ts.project_id  in (select project_id  from {{ ref('stg_projects') }}  where project_id is not null)
model.dbt_pipeline.int_timesheets_valida.compiled_sql:
-- int_timesheets_validated
-- The cross-source fact-cleaning intermediate: connects stg_timesheets to the
-- clean stg_employees / stg_projects and filters dangling FKs -> the accepted
-- fact set. This is the legitimate intermediate role (joins/references multiple
-- atomic staging models). Uses semi-joins (IN) against the clean dim-source
-- keys to avoid fan-out. No flags, no status column -- cleaning is a where
-- filter, not a flag.

select
    ts.timesheet_id,
    ts.employee_id,
    ts.project_id,
    ts.date,
    ts.hours
from "dbt_pipeline"."main_staging"."stg_timesheets" ts
where ts.employee_id in (select employee_id from "dbt_pipeline"."main_staging"."stg_employees" where employee_id is not null)
  and ts.project_id  in (select project_id  from "dbt_pipeline"."main_staging"."stg_projects"  where project_id is not null)

columns.name: timesheet_id
columns.description: Deterministic surrogate primary key; md5 of the natural key. One row per accepted timesheet.

columns.name: employee_id
columns.description: Employee foreign key; resolves to a row in stg_employees.

columns.name: project_id
columns.description: Project foreign key; resolves to a row in stg_projects.

insights.generated_description: Timestamp recording when the date occurred.

model.dbt_pipeline.rpt_project_economics.name: rpt_project_economics
model.dbt_pipeline.rpt_project_economics.description: Per-project economics report: budget, logged hours, and a derived budget_per_hour rate. Left-joins from dim_projects so all projects appear (budgeted-but-not-started projects show zero hours). budget_per_hour is meaningful only when the budget represents a labor-cost budget.
model.dbt_pipeline.rpt_project_economics.raw_sql:
-- rpt_project_economics
-- Per-project economics: combines the project budget (dim_projects) with logged
-- hours (the per-project rollup) to derive cost-per-hour. LEFT-joins from
-- dim_projects, so it includes ALL projects -- budgeted-but-not-started projects
-- appear with 0 hours, highlighting work that is funded but not yet staffed.
--
-- ASSUMPTION: budget_per_hour = budget / total_hours is meaningful only if
-- `budget` is a labor-cost budget comparable to hours. If `budget` is a total
-- project budget (incl. non-labor costs), treat budget_per_hour as indicative
-- only. budget_per_hour is NULL for projects with no hours or a NULL budget.

select
    p.project_id,
    p.project_name,
    p.budget,
    coalesce(t.total_hours, 0)::decimal(12, 2)       as total_hours,
    coalesce(t.distinct_employees, 0)::integer       as distinct_employees,
    case
        when coalesce(t.total_hours, 0) = 0 then null
        else p.budget / t.total_hours
    end::decimal(18, 4)                              as budget_per_hour
from {{ ref('dim_projects') }} p
left join {{ ref('agg_hours_per_project') }} t on t.project_id = p.project_id
order by p.project_id
model.dbt_pipeline.rpt_project_economics.compiled_sql:
-- rpt_project_economics
-- Per-project economics: combines the project budget (dim_projects) with logged
-- hours (the per-project rollup) to derive cost-per-hour. LEFT-joins from
-- dim_projects, so it includes ALL projects -- budgeted-but-not-started projects
-- appear with 0 hours, highlighting work that is funded but not yet staffed.
--
-- ASSUMPTION: budget_per_hour = budget / total_hours is meaningful only if
-- `budget` is a labor-cost budget comparable to hours. If `budget` is a total
-- project budget (incl. non-labor costs), treat budget_per_hour as indicative
-- only. budget_per_hour is NULL for projects with no hours or a NULL budget.

select
    p.project_id,
    p.project_name,
    p.budget,
    coalesce(t.total_hours, 0)::decimal(12, 2)       as total_hours,
    coalesce(t.distinct_employees, 0)::integer       as distinct_employees,
    case
        when coalesce(t.total_hours, 0) = 0 then null
        else p.budget / t.total_hours
    end::decimal(18, 4)                              as budget_per_hour
from "dbt_pipeline"."main_marts"."dim_projects" p
left join "dbt_pipeline"."main_marts"."agg_hours_per_project" t on t.project_id = p.project_id
order by p.project_id

columns.name: total_hours
columns.description: Total logged hours for this project (zero for budgeted-but-not-started projects).

columns.name: distinct_employees
columns.description: Count of distinct employees who logged hours (zero for projects with no timesheets).

columns.name: budget_per_hour
columns.description: Derived: budget / total_hours. NULL when total_hours is zero or budget is null.

model.dbt_pipeline.stg_employees.name: stg_employees
model.dbt_pipeline.stg_employees.description: Typed, deduplicated projection of raw.employees. One row per non-null employee_id (dedup survivor). Rows with a valid id but a missing name or role are retained.
model.dbt_pipeline.stg_employees.raw_sql:
with ranked as (
    select
        employee_id,
        name,
        role,
        row_number() over (partition by employee_id) as dup_rank
    from {{ source('raw', 'employees') }}
)

select
    employee_id,
    name,
    role
from ranked
where dup_rank = 1
  and employee_id is not null
model.dbt_pipeline.stg_employees.compiled_sql:
with ranked as (
    select
        employee_id,
        name,
        role,
        row_number() over (partition by employee_id) as dup_rank
    from "dbt_pipeline"."main_raw"."employees"
)

select
    employee_id,
    name,
    role
from ranked
where dup_rank = 1
  and employee_id is not null

columns.name: employee_id
columns.description: Employee identifier; non-null, exact duplicates removed.

model.dbt_pipeline.stg_projects.name: stg_projects
model.dbt_pipeline.stg_projects.description: Typed, deduplicated projection of raw.projects. One row per non-null project_id (dedup survivor by dup_rank = 1). Budget is cast to decimal; non-numeric values become NULL. Rows with a valid id but a missing or invalid attribute are retained.
model.dbt_pipeline.stg_projects.raw_sql:
with ranked as (
    select
        project_id,
        project_name,
        try_cast(budget as decimal(12, 2)) as budget,
        row_number() over (partition by project_id) as dup_rank
    from {{ source('raw', 'projects') }}
)

select
    project_id,
    project_name,
    budget
from ranked
where dup_rank = 1
  and project_id is not null
model.dbt_pipeline.stg_projects.compiled_sql:
with ranked as (
    select
        project_id,
        project_name,
        try_cast(budget as decimal(12, 2)) as budget,
        row_number() over (partition by project_id) as dup_rank
    from "dbt_pipeline"."main_raw"."projects"
)

select
    project_id,
    project_name,
    budget
from ranked
where dup_rank = 1
  and project_id is not null

columns.name: project_id
columns.description: Project identifier; non-null, exact duplicates removed.

columns.name: budget
columns.description: Project budget as decimal(12,2); nullable (non-numeric source values become NULL).

model.dbt_pipeline.stg_timesheets.name: stg_timesheets
model.dbt_pipeline.stg_timesheets.description: Typed projection of raw.timesheets with single-source validity filtering: rows missing employee_id, with an unparseable date, or with non-numeric or out-of-range hours are removed. Adds a deterministic surrogate primary key. Cross-source referential issues are resolved downstream in int_timesheets_validated.
model.dbt_pipeline.stg_timesheets.raw_sql:
with src as (
    select
        employee_id,
        project_id,
        date,
        hours,
        row_number() over (partition by employee_id, project_id, date, hours) as dup_rank
    from {{ source('raw', 'timesheets') }}
),
typed as (
    select
        employee_id,
        project_id,
        -- raw date mixes three formats (dd/mm/yyyy, ISO yyyy-mm-dd, dd-Mon-yy);
        -- try_strptime walks the format list and returns NULL if none match
        try_strptime(date, ['%d/%m/%Y', '%Y-%m-%d', '%d-%b-%y'])::date as date,
        try_cast(hours as decimal(5, 2)) as hours,
        dup_rank
    from src
)

select
    md5(
        coalesce(employee_id, '') || '|' ||
        coalesce(project_id, '')  || '|' ||
        coalesce(date::varchar, '') || '|' ||
        coalesce(hours::varchar, '')
    ) as timesheet_id,
    employee_id,
    project_id,
    date,
    hours
from typed
where dup_rank = 1                                      -- drop exact duplicates
  and employee_id is not null                            -- drop missing employee_id
  and date is not null                                    -- drop unparseable date
  and hours is not null                                   -- drop non-numeric hours
  and hours > 0 and hours <= 24                           -- drop out-of-range hours
model.dbt_pipeline.stg_timesheets.compiled_sql:
with src as (
    select
        employee_id,
        project_id,
        date,
        hours,
        row_number() over (partition by employee_id, project_id, date, hours) as dup_rank
    from "dbt_pipeline"."main_raw"."timesheets"
),
typed as (
    select
        employee_id,
        project_id,
        -- raw date mixes three formats (dd/mm/yyyy, ISO yyyy-mm-dd, dd-Mon-yy);
        -- try_strptime walks the format list and returns NULL if none match
        try_strptime(date, ['%d/%m/%Y', '%Y-%m-%d', '%d-%b-%y'])::date as date,
        try_cast(hours as decimal(5, 2)) as hours,
        dup_rank
    from src
)

select
    md5(
        coalesce(employee_id, '') || '|' ||
        coalesce(project_id, '')  || '|' ||
        coalesce(date::varchar, '') || '|' ||
        coalesce(hours::varchar, '')
    ) as timesheet_id,
    employee_id,
    project_id,
    date,
    hours
from typed
where dup_rank = 1                                      -- drop exact duplicates
  and employee_id is not null                            -- drop missing employee_id
  and date is not null                                    -- drop unparseable date
  and hours is not null                                   -- drop non-numeric hours
  and hours > 0 and hours <= 24                           -- drop out-of-range hours

search_index.description: Timesheet date; non-null. Parsed from multiple source formats.

search_index.name: hours
search_index.description: Hours worked; non-null, in range (0, 24].

search_index.name: int_timesheets_validated
search_index.description: The accepted fact set: stg_timesheets semi-joined to stg_employees and stg_projects to retain only rows whose foreign keys resolve to a real dimension row. The only cross-source model in the pipeline; feeds fct_timesheets.

search_index.name: timesheet_id
search_index.description: Deterministic surrogate primary key; md5 of the natural key. One row per accepted timesheet.

search_index.name: employee_id
search_index.description: Employee foreign key; resolves to a row in stg_employees.

search_index.name: project_id
search_index.description: Project foreign key; resolves to a row in stg_projects.

search_index.name: date
search_index.description: Parsed timesheet date; non-null.

search_index.name: hours
search_index.description: Hours worked; non-null, in range (0, 24].

search_index.name: rpt_project_economics
search_index.description: Per-project economics report: budget, logged hours, and a derived budget_per_hour rate. Left-joins from dim_projects so all projects appear (budgeted-but-not-started projects show zero hours). budget_per_hour is meaningful only when the budget represents a labor-cost budget.

search_index.name: project_id
search_index.description: Project primary key (DB-enforced).

search_index.name: project_name
search_index.description: Project name (from dim_projects).

search_index.name: budget
search_index.description: Project budget (from dim_projects); nullable.

search_index.name: total_hours
search_index.description: Total logged hours for this project (zero for budgeted-but-not-started projects).

search_index.name: distinct_employees
search_index.description: Count of distinct employees who logged hours (zero for projects with no timesheets).

search_index.name: budget_per_hour
search_index.description: Derived: budget / total_hours. NULL when total_hours is zero or budget is null.

search_index.name: stg_employees
search_index.description: Typed, deduplicated projection of raw.employees. One row per non-null employee_id (dedup survivor). Rows with a valid id but a missing name or role are retained.

search_index.name: employee_id
search_index.description: Employee identifier; non-null, exact duplicates removed.

search_index.name: name
search_index.description: Employee name; nullable.

search_index.name: role
search_index.description: Employee role; nullable.

search_index.name: stg_projects
search_index.description: Typed, deduplicated projection of raw.projects. One row per non-null project_id (dedup survivor by dup_rank = 1). Budget is cast to decimal; non-numeric values become NULL. Rows with a valid id but a missing or invalid attribute are retained.

search_index.name: project_id
search_index.description: Project identifier; non-null, exact duplicates removed.

search_index.name: project_name
search_index.description: Project name; nullable.

search_index.name: budget
search_index.description: Project budget as decimal(12,2); nullable (non-numeric source values become NULL).

search_index.name: stg_timesheets
search_index.description: Typed projection of raw.timesheets with single-source validity filtering: rows missing employee_id, with an unparseable date, or with non-numeric or out-of-range hours are removed. Adds a deterministic surrogate primary key. Cross-source referential issues are resolved downstream in int_timesheets_validated.

search_index.name: timesheet_id
search_index.description: Deterministic surrogate primary key; md5 of the natural key (employee_id, project_id, date, hours).

search_index.name: employee_id
search_index.description: Employee identifier; non-null.

search_index.name: project_id
search_index.description: Project identifier; non-null.

search_index.name: date
search_index.description: Parsed timesheet date; non-null. Coerced from multiple source formats (ISO, dd/mm/yyyy, dd-Mon-yy).

search_index.name: hours
search_index.description: Hours worked as decimal(5,2); non-null, constrained to the range (0, 24].

search_index.name: employees
search_index.description: Raw employees source (employee_id, name, role), all VARCHAR.

search_index.name: employee_id
search_index.description: Raw employee identifier (VARCHAR).

search_index.name: name
search_index.description: Raw employee name (VARCHAR).

search_index.name: role
search_index.description: Raw employee role (VARCHAR); one of a fixed set of valid roles.

search_index.name: projects
search_index.description: Raw projects source (project_id, project_name, budget), all VARCHAR.

search_index.name: project_id
search_index.description: Raw project identifier (VARCHAR).

search_index.name: project_name
search_index.description: Raw project name (VARCHAR).

search_index.name: budget
search_index.description: Raw project budget (VARCHAR); may be non-numeric, negative, or empty.

search_index.name: timesheets
search_index.description: Raw timesheets source (employee_id, project_id, date, hours), all VARCHAR.

search_index.name: employee_id
search_index.description: Raw employee foreign key (VARCHAR).

search_index.name: project_id
search_index.description: Raw project foreign key (VARCHAR).

search_index.name: date
search_index.description: Raw timesheet date (VARCHAR); mixed source formats (ISO yyyy-mm-dd, dd/mm/yyyy, dd-Mon-yy).

search_index.name: hours
search_index.description: Raw hours worked (VARCHAR).
